puzzle.title: The River's Secret
puzzle.intro: In a time of great tyranny, when the cry of newborns was met with fear, a devoted parent was divinely inspired with a plan that defied all logic. It was an act of profound faith, entrusting a precious life to the currents of a mighty river, directly into the heart of the enemy's domain. Can you identify this soul whose trust in Allah's plan saved a future prophet?
clues: I lived under the rule of a tyrant who saw a threat in the birth of every baby boy among my people.
When my child was born, my heart was filled with fear, but Allah inspired me with a way to protect him.
I was instructed to place my infant son in a basket and cast him into the river, with a promise that he would be returned to me.
My heart felt empty after I let him go, and I almost revealed my secret out of grief, but Allah strengthened me.
I sent my daughter to follow the basket from a distance, to see what would become of my son.
When the household of Fir'awn found my son and he refused all wet nurses, I was brought to him and reunited with him, becoming his nurse without them knowing I was his mother.

trial.label: What Was My Great Test?
options: Placing my infant son in the river
Making a vow to dedicate my child to God's service
Being accused of unchastity after a miraculous birth
Being left in a barren valley with my child
trial.answer: Placing my infant son in the river

outcome.label: What Was The Outcome?
options: My son spoke from the cradle to defend my honor
My son was sold into slavery in a distant land
My son was returned to me to nurse
I was given glad tidings of a son by angels in old age
My son was nearly sacrificed and ransomed with a ram
outcome.answer: My son was returned to me to nurse

puzzle.arabic: فَرَدَدْنَـٰهُ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّهِۦ كَىْ تَقَرَّ عَيْنُهَا وَلَا تَحْزَنَ وَلِتَعْلَمَ أَنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّۭ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
puzzle.verse: So We restored him to his mother that she might be content and not grieve and that she would know that the promise of Allāh is true. But most of them [i.e., the people] do not know. (28:13)
